name: Christy’s Lasagna

ingredients.item: package store-bought lasagna noodles or you can make your own.

method: Before we start the lasagna, I want to mention that you can use fresh pasta from the store or boxed lasagna noodles. Another option is to follow my homemade fettuccine recipe, but instead of running the dough through the fettuccine attachment, flatten dough to 3 thickness, instead of 1 or 2. Flour dough pieces well and place on a floured cutting board, and with a pizza cutter, cut 8-inch long, 4-inch wide strips and let dry. This recipe can serve four people and it's made in an 8-inch square casserole dish as pictured below.

In a frying pan on medium heat, add 2 T olive oil and saute onion and meat until lightly browned. Add tomatoes and 1 can of water. If you want to be fancy you can add ½ cup of red wine, or substitute the water for beef broth. Add crushed garlic, basil, and salt & pepper. If you like other spices like oregano you can also add those to the sauce- it’s up to you! Simmer for an hour.

Combine filling ingredients in a medium bowl and mix well. When the sauce has simmered, put 2 ladles of meat sauce on the bottom of an 8 inch casserole dish. Add the first layer of lasagna noodles-make rows to cover the bottom, add cheese layer and spread to cover, add layer of lasagna noodles, meat layer and repeat until you run out of ingredients or you reach the top of the casserole. End with lasagna noodles, spread remaining meat sauce to cover top, and sprinkle more mozzarella and parmesan on top. Cover with aluminum foil and bake at 350°F for 30 minutes. Remove foil and bake for 1 5 minutes or until cheese is melted and bubbly. Serves 4. Serving suggestion: fresh salad and homemade bread. Olé
